name: Sierpinski Curve Julia Sets
description: Sierpinski curve: plane continuum homeomorphic to the standard Sierpinski carpet (complement of a Cantor set of disjoint disks in a disk). Devaney studied rational maps whose Julia sets are Sierpinski curves. Results: Cantor sets of parameters yield homeomorphic Sierpinski Julia sets with distinct dynamics; Cantor sets of invariant simple closed curves on which map is conjugate to subshifts. Indecomposable continuum: cannot be union of two proper subcontinua; non-landing hairs can form indecomposable subcontinua.
mermaid:
graph TD
    Julia["Julia and Fatou sets"]
    Hairs["Devaney Hairs"]
    DefSierp["Def: Sierpinski curve\nComplement of Cantor set of disks"]
    DefIndec["Def: Indecomposable continuum\nNot union of two proper subcontinua"]
    ThmRational["Thm: Rational maps with Sierpinski J\nOne-parameter families exist"]
    ThmCantor["Thm: Cantor set of params\nHomeomorphic J, distinct dynamics"]
    ThmInvCurve["Thm: Cantor set of invariant curves\nConjugate to subshift of finite type"]
    LemHairIndec["Lem: Non-landing hairs\nClosure can be indecomposable"]
    Julia --> DefSierp
    Hairs --> LemHairIndec
    DefSierp --> DefIndec
    DefSierp --> ThmRational
    ThmRational --> ThmCantor
    ThmRational --> ThmInvCurve
    DefIndec --> LemHairIndec
